title: ArbiterOS Tool Parser Rules
description: Declarative parsing rules that classify tool calls into instruction types and security metadata. Each tool definition contains an ordered list of passes applied in sequence; later passes deep-merge over earlier results.

tool.description: Exact tool name this definition applies to.

passes.description: Ordered list of parser passes. Applied in sequence; each matching pass deep-merges its result into the accumulated output.

DefaultPass.description: Unconditional baseline result. Always matches. Typically the first pass in a definition.

RegexPass.description: Matches individual argument values against regex patterns. All matching cases within this pass are applied in order.

arg.description: Dot-notation path to the argument value to match against (e.g. 'action', 'request.kind'). If the path does not exist in the args, this case is skipped.

pattern.description: Regex matched against the string value at arg path. Evaluated case-sensitive, DOTALL off, multiline off.

NumericPass.description: Compares individual numeric argument values against thresholds. All matching cases within this pass are applied in order.

arg.description: Dot-notation path to the argument value to compare. If the path does not exist or the value is not a number, this case is skipped.

op.description: Comparison operator: eq (==), neq (!=), lt (<), lte (<=), gt (>), gte (>=).

PathPass.description: Classifies a single file path or URL argument via the path registry. One arg per pass; use multiple PathPass entries for alias args (e.g. 'path' and 'file_path'). Outputs: metadata.confidentiality, metadata.trustworthiness.

arg.description: Dot-notation path to the argument containing the file path or URL to classify (e.g. 'path', 'request.url'). Top-level args are the common case.

ShellPass.description: Delegates shell command analysis to the shell_parsers engine. Typically the first pass; subsequent passes refine its output. Outputs: instruction_type, metadata.confidentiality, metadata.trustworthiness, metadata.risk.

arg.description: Dot-notation path to the argument containing the shell command string to analyse (e.g. 'command').

Result.description: Partial or full classification result. All fields are optional to allow targeted deep-merge overrides.

custom.description: Extension point for arbitrary key-value metadata. Values are passed through unchanged into security_type.custom. Use this for fields beyond the four standard metadata fields (confidentiality, trustworthiness, risk, reversible).

Metadata.description: Security classification fields. All fields are optional to allow partial deep-merge.
